key_points: The Constitution is the supreme law of India that guides the functioning of the government
It defines the structure of government and the powers of different institutions
The Indian Constitution was drafted by the Constituent Assembly after independence
Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee
The Constitution came into effect on 26 January 1950
It guarantees Fundamental Rights to protect equality, freedom and justice
The Constitution also describes the duties and responsibilities of citizens
examples: The Right to Equality ensures that everyone is treated equally before the law
The Right to Freedom allows citizens to express opinions and move freely in the country
Courts can protect citizens if their constitutional rights are violated
Government decisions must follow the rules given in the Constitution
formulas: Protection of citizens → Fundamental Rights + Rule of Law
exam_tips: Mention the role of the Constituent Assembly while explaining the making of the Constitution
Remember the contribution of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar in drafting the Constitution
Write examples of Fundamental Rights in answers
Explain how the Constitution limits government power
